title: I Many Times Thought Peace Had Come,
content:
I many times thought peace had come,
    When peace was far away;
    As wrecked men deem they sight the land
    At centre of the sea,

    And struggle slacker, but to prove,
    As hopelessly as I,
    How many the fictitious shores
    Before the harbor lie.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled "I Many Times Thought Peace Had Come,", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
